ideas.title: Interactive Install Concierge
ideas.description: Add a guided installer that asks about target ecosystem, install scope, MCP mode, hooks, and follow-up assets before executing.
ideas.rationale: Keeps Fabric's one-shot promise but turns it into a choose-and-confirm experience for TTY users.

ideas.title: Dual-path install story
ideas.description: Split the product story into `fabric init` for automation and `fabric install` for guided onboarding.
ideas.rationale: Avoids overloading one command with conflicting expectations.

ideas.title: Capability-aware setup cards
ideas.description: Show a pre-install matrix as selectable cards/choices, then only run the stages that matter for the detected clients.
ideas.rationale: Transforms today's passive summary into an actionable setup decision surface.

key_findings: The most visible UX gap is the absence of user decisions between preflight and execution.
A wizard can become the primary product shape of init once compatibility constraints are relaxed.
challenged_assumptions: A better install UX does not require a full-screen TUI.
open_questions: Should the guided path live under `fabric install`, `fabric init --wizard`, or both?
